title: invitations — queued jobs
description: Five queued jobs. All models re-hydrated from DB on handle (no stale-payload risk). ShouldBeUnique guards on jobs whose duplicate execution would over-send.

jobs.description: Renders the outbound message + hands off to the configured mail transport. Requires access to the raw token (stashed on the Invitation model at create-time; if lost — e.g. job re-hydrates from a stale queue — the job refuses to run and marks the invitation `state=send_failed` with error_code=`token_unavailable`).

input.raw_token: string (encrypted at queue level via Sanctum PAT-style crypto)

jobs.description: Sweeps invitations whose `expires_at < now()` and are still in a non-terminal state. Transitions them to `expired`, dispatches InvitationExpired. Batched — max 500 per run to avoid blocking the queue.
After the accepted-retention window (default P90D), soft-deletes accepted + declined invitations. InvitationEvent rows are retained separately per their own retention window.

For soft-bounced invitations (transient email failures), waits for the transport-recommended retry delay + resends. Refused for hard bounces + complaints (terminal). Tracks attempt count on the InvitationEvent metadata to avoid infinite retry.

Handles the queued portion of mail-transport webhook processing. The webhook controller validates signature synchronously then enqueues this job to write InvitationEvent rows + trigger state transitions. Idempotent on (signal_id, transport) — the DB unique index catches duplicates.

invitations.description: Outbound message dispatch + retry. Isolated because mail transport slowness must not stall other work.

retention.description: Batched cleanup jobs. Runs off-hours per schedule.json.

webhooks.description: Inbound webhook processing. Isolated because mail-transport delivery signal spikes (bulk resends) must not stall admin-triggered sends.
